description: M11 PR5 code-locate golden set (task 65, decision 107). Recall@K acceptance for `memhub eval locate` over memhub's own tree via the sibling code index. Matchers run case-insensitive substring checks against each locate hit's repo-relative `path` (and optional `symbol`), so queries survive line-range shifts and chunk re-splits. The acceptance bar is decision 107's deliberately-lossy one: the EXPECTED FILE in the top K, not a perfect chunk — precision is recovered by the agent's real Read afterward. `kind: match` queries contribute to Recall@1/@K; `kind: empty` queries are nonsense safety probes. NOTE: plain hybrid locate has no score floor, so it returns the top-N by cosine for ANY string — empty probes only reject under `--rerank --min-rerank-score <F>`, which is precisely the floor signal this set exists to measure. Queries are intentionally plain-English 'where does X live' phrasings that avoid the symbol vocabulary, giving the cross-encoder reranker headroom to prove (or not) on code above the FTS+vector fusion baseline.

queries.query: compute the cosine similarity between two embedding vectors
queries.notes: cosine_similarity helper in the locate fusion path.

queries.query: turn a free text query into a quoted FTS5 AND expression
read a short clipped excerpt of a chunk body from disk
decide if the local database and the synced drive copy have diverged
resolve the canonical remote folder path for cross machine sync
delete superseded cargo build artifacts to reclaim disk space
call the anthropic count tokens endpoint to calibrate the tokenizer ratio
queries.notes: the one network-touching command; cl100k to real ratio.

queries.query: list the git tracked files filtered through the deny list
check whether a file path is excluded by the ignore patterns
record the full prompt size of the first turn of a session
queries.notes: baseline_input_tokens scraped from the first usage line.

queries.query: harness that measures how often the expected row lands in the top results
queries.notes: run_retrieval Recall@K harness (the sibling of this very harness).

queries.query: split rust source into chunks by function struct and impl block
bring the code index in line with the current working tree
reorder candidate results by relevance with a cross encoder model
turn a piece of text into a fixed length embedding vector
blend keyword and vector scores and penalize stale facts during recall
queries.notes: recall fusion with stale penalty (distinct from locate's no-decay fusion).

queries.query: apply pending database schema migrations idempotently on open
drop and rebuild the sibling index when the schema version mismatches
queries.notes: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS + version-mismatch DROP+REBUILD.

queries.query: zxqv plorptin wqxz frobnicate gzorp
queries.notes: Pure nonsense — should reject under a rerank floor; documents that fusion-only locate has no nonsense gate.

queries.query: asdfghjkl qwertyuiop mnbvcxz lkjhgfdsa
